description: Implement `packages/@ortho/event-bus` — a pluggable event bus with EventBridge+FIFO-SQS (production) and Redis Streams (local/CI) drivers. Services call createEventBus(), publish(), subscribe(), and start(); the driver is selected at runtime via EVENT_BUS_DRIVER. Multiple handlers per event type are called in series; subscribe() throws if called after start(); start() with zero subscriptions logs a warning. OrthoEvent carries optional correlation_id, causation_id, and schema_version fields.

userStories.title: Package scaffold, core types, and EventBus class
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the @ortho/event-bus package structure, core TypeScript types, and the EventBus class implementation (without drivers) so services can import a typed interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `packages/@ortho/event-bus/` with: `package.json` (name `@ortho/event-bus`, type `module`, main/types pointing to `dist/index`), `tsconfig.json` extending workspace base, `src/index.ts` re-exporting all public symbols

Create `src/types.ts` exporting: `OrthoEvent` interface with fields `event_type: string`, `entity_type?: string`, `entity_id?: string`, `payload: Record<string, unknown>`, `correlation_id?: string`, `causation_id?: string`, `schema_version?: string`; `EventHandler` type `(event: OrthoEvent) => Promise<void>`; `EventBus` interface with `publish`, `subscribe`, `start`, `stop`; `Driver` interface with `publish`, `start(subscriptions: Map<string, EventHandler[]>)`, `stop`

Create `src/event-bus.ts` implementing `EventBus`: `subscribe(eventType, handler)` appends handler to a `Map<string, EventHandler[]>` (multiple handlers per event type called in series); throws `Error('subscribe() called after start()')` if called after `start()` was invoked; `publish(event)` delegates to the injected driver; `start()` logs a warning via `console.warn` and resolves if zero subscriptions registered, otherwise delegates to driver passing the subscriptions map; `stop()` delegates to driver; tracks started state with a private boolean flag

Unit tests in `src/event-bus.test.ts` using a `MockDriver` stub: subscribe before start registers handler; subscribe after start throws; publish calls driver.publish with correct event; start with zero subscriptions calls console.warn and resolves without calling driver.start; start with subscriptions calls driver.start with correct map; multiple handlers for same event type are both present in the map; stop calls driver.stop
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: createEventBus factory and MockDriver
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a createEventBus() factory that reads EVENT_BUS_DRIVER to select the concrete driver, and a MockDriver for use in service unit tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/factory.ts` exporting `EventBusOptions { driver?: Driver }` and `createEventBus(options?: EventBusOptions): EventBus`; when `options.driver` is provided, use it directly; otherwise read `EVENT_BUS_DRIVER` env var: `'eventbridge'` → instantiate `EventBridgeDriver`, `'redis'` → instantiate `RedisStreamsDriver`, absent/other → throw `Error('EVENT_BUS_DRIVER must be set to "eventbridge" or "redis"')`

Create `src/drivers/mock.ts` exporting `MockDriver` implementing `Driver`: records all `publish()` calls in a public `published: OrthoEvent[]` array; `start()` and `stop()` are no-ops returning resolved promises; export as part of the public API from `src/index.ts`

Unit tests for factory: `options.driver` provided → returns EventBus using that driver; `EVENT_BUS_DRIVER=redis` → no throw (driver instantiated); `EVENT_BUS_DRIVER=eventbridge` → no throw (driver instantiated); missing env var → throws with descriptive message
All public symbols (`createEventBus`, `EventBus`, `OrthoEvent`, `EventHandler`, `Driver`, `MockDriver`) are re-exported from `src/index.ts`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: EventBridgeDriver — FIFO SQS publish and consume
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the production driver to publish events via AWS EventBridge and consume them from a FIFO SQS queue with at-least-once delivery and DLQ support.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/drivers/eventbridge.ts` exporting `EventBridgeDriver` implementing `Driver`; constructor reads env vars `EVENT_BRIDGE_BUS_NAME` and `SQS_QUEUE_URL` (throws if either is missing); uses `@aws-sdk/client-eventbridge` for publish and `@aws-sdk/client-sqs` for consume

`publish(event)` calls `PutEvents` with `Source: 'ortho'`, `EventBusName: EVENT_BRIDGE_BUS_NAME`, `DetailType: event.event_type`, `Detail: JSON.stringify(event)`; uses FIFO queue — sets `MessageGroupId: event.entity_id ?? event.event_type` and `MessageDeduplicationId: a UUID` on each `SendMessage` call (Note: EventBridge does not natively support FIFO SQS as a target; use `@aws-sdk/client-sqs` `SendMessage` directly with the FIFO queue URL instead of PutEvents, sending the full serialized OrthoEvent as the `MessageBody`)

`start(subscriptions)` launches an SQS long-poll loop: `ReceiveMessage` with `MaxNumberOfMessages: 10`, `WaitTimeSeconds: 20`; parse `MessageBody` as `OrthoEvent`; look up all handlers for `event_type` from subscriptions map; if no handlers, `DeleteMessage` immediately; call handlers in series — on all success `DeleteMessage`; on any handler throw do NOT delete (SQS redelivers until `maxReceiveCount` reached, then goes to DLQ); loop exits when `stop()` sets a shutdown flag after the current receive completes
`stop()` sets shutdown flag; returns a promise that resolves after the current poll iteration completes (use a `stopped` promise pattern)

Unit tests with mocked `@aws-sdk/client-sqs`: publish sends correct MessageBody and MessageGroupId; consume with matching handler calls handler and deletes message; consume with no registered handler deletes message without calling any handler; consume with handler that throws does not delete message; stop() causes loop to exit
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: RedisStreamsDriver — XADD publish, XREADGROUP consume, and consumer-lag check
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the local/CI driver to publish events to Redis Streams and consume them via consumer groups, with DLQ routing after 3 failed deliveries and a consumer-lag warning.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/drivers/redis-streams.ts` exporting `RedisStreamsDriver` implementing `Driver`; constructor accepts `{ redisUrl: string; consumerGroup: string }` (factory reads `REDIS_URL` and `EVENT_BUS_CONSUMER_GROUP`); uses `ioredis`
`publish(event)` calls `XADD stream:{event.event_type} MAXLEN ~ 10000 * event_type <val> entity_type <val> ...` (serialize all OrthoEvent fields as flat string key-value pairs)

`start(subscriptions)` creates one `XREADGROUP` loop per subscribed event type: `XGROUP CREATE stream:{eventType} {GROUP} $ MKSTREAM` (ignore BUSYGROUP error); consumer ID = `{GROUP}-{hostname}-{pid}`; loop: `XREADGROUP GROUP {GROUP} {consumerId} COUNT 10 BLOCK 2000 STREAMS stream:{eventType} >`; on message: deserialize → call handlers in series → `XACK` on success; on handler throw: no XACK; check `XPENDING` count for this message — if delivery count ≥ 3, `XADD stream:dlq * ...` then `XACK` to prevent infinite retry; consumer-lag check: after each read, `XPENDING stream:{eventType} {GROUP} - + 1` to get oldest pending — if pending count exceeds 1000, `console.warn` with lag warning; loop exits when stop flag is set after BLOCK timeout (≤ 2s)
`stop()` sets shutdown flag; waits up to 3s for all XREADGROUP loops to exit; calls `redis.quit()`

Unit tests with mocked ioredis client: publish calls XADD with correct stream name and MAXLEN; start creates consumer group (ignores BUSYGROUP error); message received calls handlers in series and XACKs; handler throw results in no XACK; after 3 delivery attempts moves to DLQ stream and XACKs; pending count > 1000 logs consumer-lag warning
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
